# services.quicktun

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.quicktun`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.quicktun` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | QuickTun tunnels. See <http://wiki.ucis.nl/QuickTun> for more information about available options. |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.localAddress` | `null or string` | IP address or hostname of the local end. |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.localPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Local UDP port. |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.privateKey` | `null or string` | Local secret key in hexadecimal form. ::: {.warning} This option is deprecated. Please use {var}`services.quicktun.<name>.privateKeyFile` instead. ::: ::: {.note} Not needed when {var}`services.quicktun.<name>.protocol` is set to `raw`. ::: |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.privateKeyFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to file containing local secret key in binary or hexadecimal form. ::: {.note} Not needed when {var}`services.quicktun.<name>.protocol` is set to `raw`. ::: |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.protocol` | `one of "raw", "nacl0", "nacltai", "salty"` | Which protocol to use. |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.publicKey` | `null or string` | Remote public key in hexadecimal form. ::: {.note} Not needed when {var}`services.quicktun.<name>.protocol` is set to `raw`. ::: |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.remoteAddress` | `string` | IP address or hostname of the remote end (use `0.0.0.0` for a floating/dynamic remote endpoint). |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.remoteFloat` | `integer between 0 and 1 (both inclusive) or boolean convertible to it` | Whether to allow the remote address and port to change when properly encrypted packets are received. |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.remotePort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Remote UDP port |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.timeWindow` | `unsigned integer, meaning >=0` | Allowed time window for first received packet in seconds (positive number allows packets from history) |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.tunMode` | `integer between 0 and 1 (both inclusive) or boolean convertible to it` | Whether to operate in tun (IP) or tap (Ethernet) mode. |
| `services.quicktun.<name>.upScript` | `null or strings concatenated with "\n"` | Run specified command or script after the tunnel device has been opened. |
